data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **TestProject.csproj changes:**
   - Replace `xunit` (v2) with `xunit.v3` (v3)
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (v3 doesn't need a separate runner package; test projects are executables)
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (not needed for v3, or might need a different approach)
   - Replace `Xunit.Combinatorial` with `xunit.v3.assert.combinatorial` or similar - actually, let me check. In xUnit v3, combinatorial support might be built-in or have a different package.
   - Handle `Xunit.StaFact` similarly, as it likely has a v3 equivalent package
   - Update the project SDK or set `OutputType` to `Exe` since v3 test projects are executables

2. **global.json changes:**
   - Update the runner configuration from `"runner": "VSTest"` to work with the xUnit v3 runner based on Microsoft.Testing.Platform, or remove it entirely

3. **Code changes:**
   - The `using Xunit;` namespace remains unchanged
   - Attributes like `[StaFact]` need to be updated to their v3 equivalents
   - `[CombinatorialData]` also needs checking for v3 compatibility

I need to look into the actual xUnit v3 package names and what the migration path looks like. The main package is `xunit.v3` instead of `xunit`, and since v3 test projects are executables, you don't need `xunit.runner.visualstudio` or `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` anymore. I should verify whether `Xunit.Combinatorial` and `Xunit.StaFact` have v3-compatible versions available. With xUnit v3, the runner is built-in so I don't need separate test runner packages, and test projects need to be executables rather than class libraries. I should also update the global.json configuration to use "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" instead of "VSTest" for v3. Let me check NuGet to find the exact versions of `Xunit.Combinatorial` and other v3-compatible packages.

arguments.command: curl -s 'https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/xunit.v3/index.json' | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; [print(x) for x in v[-5:]]"
curl -s 'https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/xunit.combinatorial/index.json' | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; [print(x) for x in v[-10:]]"
curl -s 'https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/xunit.stafact/index.json' | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; [print(x) for x in v[-10:]]"

data.reasoningText:
Now I have the version information:
- `xunit.v3`: latest stable is `3.2.2`
- `Xunit.Combinatorial`: for xUnit v3, the version is `2.0.24` (2.x line targets v3)
- `Xunit.StaFact`: for xUnit v3, the version is `2.1.7` or `3.0.13`. Let me check which one targets xUnit v3.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The versioning of these packages:
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` 2.x → likely targets xUnit v3


- `Xunit.StaFact` - I need to verify which version actually targets xUnit v3, since the versioning scheme might not be straightforward. Let me check the package dependencies to figure this out.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>Xunit.Combinatorial</id>
    <version>2.0.24</version>
    <title>Combinatorial testing with Xunit</title>
    <authors>Andrew Arnott</authors>
    <license type="expression">MS-PL</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/MS-PL</licenseUrl>
    <readme>README.md</readme>
    <projectUrl>https://github.com/aarnott/Xunit.Combinatorial</projectUrl>
    <description>Adds pairwise and combinatorial testing to Xunit. When combined with Xunit's [Theory] attribute, generates test cases that run a test under many conditions that you control via test method parameters.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.Combinatorial/releases/tag/v2.0.24</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>© Andrew Arnott. All rights reserved.</copyright>
    <tags>xunit combinatorial pairwise testing</tags>
    <repository type="git" url="https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.Combinatorial" branch="refs/heads/v2.0" commit="b79a39092ccb0ae6dec8c840e4fb075f7def5cb8" />
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETFramework4.7.2">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="1.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard2.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="1.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
  </metadata>
</package>---
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>Xunit.StaFact</id>
    <version>3.0.13</version>
    <authors>Andrew Arnott</authors>
    <license type="expression">MS-PL</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/MS-PL</licenseUrl>
    <readme>README.md</readme>
    <projectUrl>https://aarnott.github.io/Xunit.StaFact/</projectUrl>
    <description>Run your xunit-based tests on an STA thread with the WPF Dispatcher, a WinForms SynchronizationContext, or even a cross-platform generic UI thread emulation with a SynchronizationContext that keeps code running on a "main thread" for that test.

Simply use [WpfFact], [WinFormsFact], [StaFact] or the cross-platform [UIFact] on your test method to run your test under conditions that most closely match the main thread in your application.

Theory variants of these attributes allow for parameterized testing. Check out the xunit.combinatorial nuget package for pairwise or combinatorial testing with theories.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.StaFact/releases/tag/v3.0.13</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>© Andrew Arnott. All rights reserved.</copyright>
    <tags>STA xunit testing unit WPF WinForms testing</tags>
    <repository type="git" url="https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.StaFact" branch="refs/heads/main" commit="9fbe278efe7bafd2ce7618b6d6ae0baaa08cce4d" />
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETFramework4.7.2">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-macos14.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-windows7.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard2.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="3.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
    <frameworkReferences>
      <group targetFramework="net8.0" />
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-macos14.0" />
      <group targetFramework="net8.0-windows7.0">
<exited with exit code 0>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>Xunit.Combinatorial</id>
    <version>2.0.24</version>
    <title>Combinatorial testing with Xunit</title>
    <authors>Andrew Arnott</authors>
    <license type="expression">MS-PL</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/MS-PL</licenseUrl>
    <readme>README.md</readme>
    <projectUrl>https://github.com/aarnott/Xunit.Combinatorial</projectUrl>
    <description>Adds pairwise and combinatorial testing to Xunit. When combined with Xunit's [Theory] attribute, generates test cases that run a test under many conditions that you control via test method parameters.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.Combinatorial/releases/tag/v2.0.24</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>© Andrew Arnott. All rights reserved.</copyright>
    <tags>xunit combinatorial pairwise testing</tags>
    <repository type="git" url="https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.Combinatorial" branch="refs/heads/v2.0" commit="b79a39092ccb0ae6dec8c840e4fb075f7def5cb8" />
    <dependencies>
      <group targetFramework=".NETFramework4.7.2">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="1.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
      <group targetFramework=".NETStandard2.0">
        <dependency id="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" version="1.0.0" exclude="Build,Analyzers" />
      </group>
    </dependencies>
  </metadata>
</package>---
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<package xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/packaging/2013/05/nuspec.xsd">
  <metadata>
    <id>Xunit.StaFact</id>
    <version>3.0.13</version>
    <authors>Andrew Arnott</authors>
    <license type="expression">MS-PL</license>
    <licenseUrl>https://licenses.nuget.org/MS-PL</licenseUrl>
    <readme>README.md</readme>
    <projectUrl>https://aarnott.github.io/Xunit.StaFact/</projectUrl>
    <description>Run your xunit-based tests on an STA thread with the WPF Dispatcher, a WinForms SynchronizationContext, or even a cross-platform generic UI thread emulation with a SynchronizationContext that keeps code running on a "main thread" for that test.

Simply use [WpfFact], [WinFormsFact], [StaFact] or the cross-platform [UIFact] on your test method to run your test under conditions that most closely match the main thread in your application.

Theory variants of these attributes allow for parameterized testing. Check out the xunit.combinatorial nuget package for pairwise or combinatorial testing with theories.</description>
    <releaseNotes>https://github.com/AArnott/Xunit.StaFact/releases/tag/v3.0.13</releaseNotes>
    <copyright>© Andrew Arnott. All rights reserved.</copyright>
    <tags>STA xunit testing unit WPF WinForms testing</tags>

Now I have the version info. Let me apply the migration.

**Key changes:**
- `xunit` → `xunit.v3` (3.2.2)
- Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (v3 is self-hosted)
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` 1.6.24 → 2.0.24 (v3-compatible)
- `Xunit.StaFact` 1.1.11 → 3.0.13 (v3-compatible)
- Add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` (v3 test projects are executables)
- Update `global.json` runner

<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Xunit.Combinatorial" Version="1.6.24" />
    <PackageReference Include="Xunit.StaFact" Version="1.1.11" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:

    <TargetFramework>net8.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.v3" Version="3.2.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Xunit.Combinatorial" Version="2.0.24" />
    <PackageReference Include="Xunit.StaFact" Version="3.0.13" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need:

- `xunit.v3` latest stable: `3.2.2`
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` 2.0.24: depends on `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` >= 1.0.0 → compatible with xUnit v3
- `Xunit.StaFact` 3.0.13: depends on `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` >= 3.0.0 → compatible with xUnit v3

Now let me plan the migration:

1. **TestProject.csproj**:
   - Swap out the xunit v2 package for xunit.v3, and remove the test runner and SDK packages since they're no longer needed
   - Bump Xunit.Combinatorial to version 2.0.24 and Xunit.StaFact to 3.0.13
   - Add `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` because xUnit v3 test projects compile as executables
   - Update global.json to use Microsoft.Testing.Platform instead of VSTest
   - The test file itself should keep the same `using Xunit;` namespace, and the `[Theory, CombinatorialData]` attribute pattern remains unchanged since the combinatorial package still exposes those attributes through the standard Xunit namespace

, the test code should compile without changes. I should also verify that xUnit v3 requires the `net8.0-windows` target framework since `[StaFact]` depends on Windows-specific STA thread support. Let me go ahead and make these updates.

data.content: The build error is a platform issue — `net8.0-windows` requires the Windows Desktop framework which isn't available on this Linux machine. This isn't related to the migration (the original v2 project had the same TFM). Let me verify the original would also fail, and then do a restore-only check:
data.reasoningText:
The build fails because the `net8.0-windows` TFM requires the `Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App` framework reference, which is not available on this Linux machine. The `Xunit.StaFact` 3.0.13 has dependencies targeting `net8.0-windows7.0`, but the package also supports `net8.0` and `.NETStandard2.0`.

However, looking at the original project, it was already targeting `net8.0-windows`. That means the original project also wouldn't build on this Linux machine. The error is not related to my migration - it's a platform limitation.


Let me check if the original project had the same constraint.

data.content:
Package restore succeeds. The build error (`NETSDK1073: FrameworkReference 'Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App' was not recognized`) is because this Linux environment lacks the Windows Desktop SDK — the original v2 project with `net8.0-windows` would have the same issue.

Here's a summary of all changes made:

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| **TestProject.csproj** | `xunit` 2.9.3 → `xunit.v3` 3.2.2 |
| | Removed `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (v3 is self-hosted) |
| | Removed `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (not needed for v3) |
| | `Xunit.Combinatorial` 1.6.24 → 2.0.24 |
| | `Xunit.StaFact` 1.1.11 → 3.0.13 |
| | Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` |
| **global.json** | Runner: `VSTest` → `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` |
| **CombinatorialTests.cs** | No changes needed (API is compatible) |
